notes: Seed crystal for the Invariant Ontology (CFS-002). status=proposed until ratified via canonization; ratified_source marks statements drawn verbatim from ratified Polity documents.

invariants.statement: Reasoning discovers invariants.

provenance.source: Invariant Intelligence FCR v0.1, Principle I

invariants.statement: Invariants constitute knowledge.

provenance.source: Invariant Intelligence FCR v0.1, Principle II

invariants.statement: Knowledge precedes inference.

provenance.source: Invariant Intelligence FCR v0.1, Principle III

invariants.statement: Compressed expertise is a reusable computational primitive.

provenance.source: Invariant Intelligence FCR v0.1, Principle IV

invariants.statement: Adaptive systems compose validated primitives rather than rediscover them.

provenance.source: Invariant Intelligence FCR v0.1, Principle V

invariants.statement: Civilizational progress is the progressive accumulation of validated invariants.

provenance.source: Invariant Intelligence FCR v0.1, Principle VII

invariants.statement: Reasoning is a process; knowledge is the product.
An invariant does not change with its domain; its context does.
Explainability is retrieval of provenance, not post-hoc rationalisation.
A superseded belief's provenance is itself knowledge.
Personhood precedes identity.

provenance.source: Chrysalis Foundation bundle v0.1, Appendix A

invariants.statement: Standing follows action.

provenance.source: Chrysalis Foundation bundle v0.1, Appendix A

invariants.statement: Authority follows standing.

provenance.source: Chrysalis Foundation bundle v0.1, Appendix A

invariants.statement: Delegation never removes accountability.

provenance.source: Chrysalis Foundation bundle v0.1, Appendix A

invariants.statement: Authority may be delegated; sovereignty may not.
Sovereignty remains exclusively with human citizens.
An agent may exercise delegated authority but may never create new authority.
Standing is confidence in the veracity of declarations, not reputation.
Citizens are responsible for veracity, not for predicting consequences of truthful information.
Permanent and unlimited delegation is prohibited.
Humans define semantics; AI optimizes implementation.
Canonical status requires human ratification.
Constitutional memory is append-only; supersession replaces deletion.
Identifiers that re-identify a subject never leave the server.

provenance.source: Identity & Access Spine, T0 tier rule

invariants.statement: Preserve before replace.
Extend before recreate.
Compose before specialize.
Discover invariants before designing abstractions.
Compress expertise before automating it.
Separate architecture from rendering.
Separate reasoning from inference.
Treat invariants as first-class computational primitives.
Adaptive systems render; canonical systems govern.
Every evolution must strengthen the invariant ontology.
Three similar lines of code beat a premature abstraction.
One authoritative location per concern.
A parallel implementation of an existing capability is a defect.

provenance.source: CLAUDE.md, Identity & Access Spine; CFS-009 Law II

invariants.statement: Never guess a value that cannot be verified from source.
Additive schema change precedes destructive schema change.
Every state transition of record emits a receipt.
A failure in the provenance chain is escalated, never silent.
Agency increases through progressive disclosure.

provenance.source: Chrysalis Foundation bundle v0.1, Appendix A

invariants.statement: Experience should adapt while architecture remains invariant.

provenance.source: Chrysalis Foundation bundle v0.1, Appendix A

invariants.statement: Rendering is contextual; primitives are constitutional.

provenance.source: Chrysalis Foundation bundle v0.1, Appendix A

invariants.statement: Experience depth ascends one step at a time.
Derivative content renders inside the context that produced it.
The operator reads posture at a glance; state must be legible before it is precise.
Capability is composition over validated primitives.
A capability's trust is bounded by its least-trusted member.
Consequence forecasting precedes irreversible action.
Repair cost, uncertainty, and reversibility bound permissible autonomy.

provenance.source: CFS-006a Risk stage; PSC-001 recovery-velocity classes

invariants.statement: Observed consequence is the final validator of forecast capability.
Standing converts consequence into constitutional capital.
New knowledge from consequence feeds the next intent.
Civilization accumulates invariants, not information.
Knowledge enables capability.
Capability creates consequence.
The Polity defines why; AgentiQ defines how; Chrysalis defines how it evolves.
Invariants themselves accrue Standing.
Truth is established through validation within a domain of applicability, not by popularity.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XII — Truth, Standing and Reach

invariants.statement: Standing expresses constitutional confidence in an invariant and shall never be interpreted as a measure of truth.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XII — Truth, Standing and Reach

invariants.statement: Reach measures adoption rather than validity; constitutional knowledge preserves the distinction between Truth, Standing, and Reach.

provenance.source: CFS-009 Law XII — Truth, Standing and Reach

invariants.statement: Personhood precedes individualization. Individualization precedes standing. Identity is an optional derivative of individualization rather than a prerequisite for constitutional participation.
Individualization is the establishment of a constitutional subject capable of accruing Standing without that subject being identifiable.

Individualization is defined by continuity, not disclosure — a constitutional subject's persistence across time and interaction is what allows validated action to accumulate into Standing, independent of whether that subject is ever identified.
Identity is a branch of individualization, not its gate; it yields reputation, not standing.

Individualization's continuity shall be maintained without requiring re-identification by any party, including the server — a target which current server-internal (T0) implementations approximate but do not define the limit of.
The protagonist's appearance, wardrobe, and age remain identical across every segment of a production.
Lighting language, colour palette, and camera language remain identical across every segment of a production.
Aspect ratio, rendering quality, and typography remain identical across every segment of a production.
Every segment shares the same protagonist, the same constitutional world, and one continuous timeline.
A recurring visual motif and a recurring constitutional symbol persist across every segment.
Every visual metaphor corresponds to one or more grounding invariants; no visual element implies a principle absent from the originating collection.
Each segment begins from the final semantic state of the preceding segment, preserving character position, environmental state, lighting direction, and active motifs.
The production opens by establishing the subject's state before any constitutional change.
An inciting realization introduces the constitutional principle the production will test.
The subject moves through constitutional tension between the old order and the principle introduced.
The tension resolves — the constitutional principle prevails, fails, or is qualified.
The subject's constitutional transformation is shown as the production's final state.
Every invariant class shall define its composition law before entering canonical status.
The runtime is closed; the ontology is open — new capability enters by ratifying a new invariant class, not by rewriting the engine.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §3 — the Open/Closed Principle applied constitutionally

invariants.statement: Every experience is the coherent composition of multiple invariant classes operating simultaneously within a shared constitutional context.
Knowledge composes locally; experiences succeed globally through coherence.
Every constitutional experience is the multiplicative composition of independently verifiable invariant fields; changing any field changes the whole experience.

provenance.source: Law XV — Compositional Fields (discovered by EXP-002's first production brief, 2026-07-04)

invariants.statement: Invariant fields are locally independent and globally dependent; a compositional failure can live purely in an interaction while every field is individually correct.

provenance.source: Law XV / CFS-013 §6 — evidenced by the terminal-beat defect caught by CFS-014

invariants.statement: A composed block that spans invariant families is scaffolding; it dissolves into its constituent classes as those classes are ratified, and no code may treat the mixed block as constitutionally final.

provenance.source: Law XV class-purity corollary — the v1 Continuity Block (CFS-011 backlog)

invariants.statement: Constitutional evolution occurs when the constitutional system detects a coherent pattern that no individual field reveals, and that pattern is subsequently ratified by human constitutional authority.

provenance.source: CFS-009 constitutional principle — Constitutional Evolution (first instance: Law XV, revealed by CFS-014, ratified 2026-07-04)

invariants.statement: Consequences are emergent properties of composition; they arise from interactions between fields, not from fields in isolation.

provenance.source: CFS-006a §7 — the compositional grounding of consequence engineering (type specimen: the terminal-beat defect)

invariants.statement: Consequence engineering is the discipline of composing fields whose interaction reliably produces desirable consequences — architecture, not forecasting.
Constitutional fields shall compose according to a constitutionally valid sequence; correct components arranged in an invalid sequence do not constitute a coherent experience.

provenance.source: Law XV sequencing corollary — Constitutional Sequencing (CFS-013 §7, ratified 2026-07-05). Proposed as INV-075; issued as 078 — 075 was already taken and the append-only rule forbids renumbering. Type specimen: the EXP-002 terminal-beat defect re-read as a temporal-correctness failure.

invariants.statement: Local, relational, and temporal correctness are distinct properties: a field may be internally valid, agree with the others, and still occur out of constitutional order — each failure class requires its own validation.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §7 — the three-correctness taxonomy (local = field integrity, relational = composition, temporal = sequencing)

invariants.statement: Constitutional coherence is not merely the presence of correct components, but the emergence of a valid whole through correct composition and correct sequence.

provenance.source: CFS-009 constitutional principle — Constitutional Emergence (the bridge between Law XV and Law XIV, ratified 2026-07-05)

invariants.statement: An Invariant Field is a coherent domain of interacting invariants that governs the behaviour of a system; fields are independently verifiable, composable, and give rise to emergent phenomena through their interaction with other fields.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the universal Invariant Field definition, generalized from the constitutional-experience-scoped reading of Law XV after observing the same compositional mathematics governs biology, engineering, operating systems, civilization, and intelligence, not only constitutional experience (ratified 2026-07-06, operator + agent co-authored).

invariants.statement: Every invariant field shall be coherent (governs one concern), independent (verifiable in isolation), composable (contributes to system behaviour only through composition), versioned (evolves by supersession), and traceable (every observable behaviour can identify the fields that composed it).
Every coherent system is the composition of independently verifiable invariant fields; constitutional experiences are one expression of this general principle.

provenance.source: Law XV, generalized 2026-07-06 (CFS-009 amendment). Broadens the SCOPE of inv.experience.072, not its content — 072 remains constitutionally recorded and true as the constitutional-scoped instance of this universal law.

invariants.statement: An Invariant Collection is a curated grouping of invariants; an Invariant Field is a functional role in composition that one or more collections occupy — a field is a role, not a container.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the Field/Collection/Namespace distinction, keeping storage, classification, and execution unconflated.

invariants.statement: Invariant fields may be natural (discovered — e.g. gravitation, genetics, thermodynamics) or constitutional (ratified — e.g. governance, protocols, experience architectures); both obey the same compositional mathematics, differing only in origin.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — natural vs. constitutional invariant fields.

invariants.statement: The Semantic Field defines what may be constitutionally expressed — the principles, constraints, meanings, and invariants an experience draws on; without it there is no constitutional meaning.
The Narrative Field defines when constitutional meaning is revealed — governing sequence, transformation, progression, and resolution; without it there is no constitutional journey.
The Style Field defines how constitutional meaning is perceived — governing visual, linguistic, and aesthetic continuity; without it there is no constitutional identity.

The Experience Field defines how constitutional meaning is encountered by a constitutional subject — governing personalization, agency, rendering, pacing, and orchestration; without it there is no constitutional interaction.
The Context Field defines the circumstances under which every other field operates — context determines applicability, never truth; without it there is no constitutional judgment.

provenance.source: CFS-002 §2a — the five constitutional fields (5/5). Namespace remains unpopulated — Law XV's 'named slot' — pending ratified context invariants.

invariants.statement: A constitutional experience is an emergent property of field composition — it is not stored, it is composed.
Every constitutional experience shall be decomposable back into the fields and invariants from which it was composed.

Temporal correctness is graded, not boolean: an experience's coherence varies with its composition order; the designed sequence is a coherence maximum, and alternative orderings may realise distinct coherent experiences at different coherence values.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §7 empirical refinement — EXP-002 sequencing control arm, 2026-07-05: reversed-order cut degraded coherence without zeroing it; operator insight that sequencing scores rather than passes/fails.

invariants.statement: Local coherence survives a global sequence violation: relational correctness is pairwise-local while temporal correctness is global, so adjacent units still agree even when the whole no longer unfolds in constitutional order.

provenance.source: CFS-013 §7 empirical refinement — EXP-002 sequencing control arm, 2026-07-05: the reversed cut's adjacent original pairs retained pairwise coherence while the global arc broke; the three-correctness taxonomy's rows are independently damageable and therefore independently validatable.
